project_type: Jupyter Notebook ML project (Bengali cyberbullying multi-label classification)
build_system: None (Jupyter notebooks, designed for Kaggle with T4 x2 GPUs)
test_framework: None (validation is ensuring valid JSON and valid Python AST in all code cells)
build_command: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-lightweight-v4.ipynb')); json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb')); print('Both notebooks are valid JSON')"
verification_instructions: 1. Run build_command to verify JSON validity. 2. Run test_command to verify all Python cells parse. 3. Grep for old dataset references to confirm they are removed. 4. Verify MERGE_TROLL_INSULT is removed. 5. Verify new hyperparameters match specification.
setup_instructions: No setup needed - these are .ipynb JSON files edited directly
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET mode. Notebooks are designed to run on Kaggle, not locally. Validation is AST-level only.
contribution_requirements: Commit changes to both notebooks with a descriptive message.
key_patterns: Both notebooks follow the same pattern: Config class in Section 2, data loading in Section 3, training loop with SWA/R-Drop. Track 1 has CharCNN+FastText+TextCNN+BiGRU+Attention (~10M params). Track 2 has BanglaBERT+mean-pool+linear (~110M params). Both derive neutral as NOT(any toxic).
directory_structure: Root contains two .ipynb notebooks and one CSV dataset file. The .agents/ directory has task state.
